---
format:
  pdf:
    lang: en
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    keep-tex: false
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    mainfont: "Arial"
    fontenc: "T1"
    inputenc: "utf8"
    fontsize: 10pt
    title-block-style: none
    colorlinks: true
    urlcolor: blue
    linkcolor: blue
    geometry: "top=1.5cm, bottom=1.5cm, left=2cm, right=2cm"
    papersize: letter
    microtype: true
    linestretch: 1.0
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \usepackage[normalem]{ulem}
        \newcommand{\cvsection}[1]{%
          \vspace{-1.5em}
          \section*{\fontsize{11}{13}\selectfont\underline{\makebox[\linewidth][l]{#1}}}%
          \vspace{-1.3em}
        }
        \usepackage{setspace}
        \singlespacing
        \usepackage{enumitem}
        \setlist{itemsep=2pt, parsep=0pt, topsep=0pt, partopsep=0pt}
---

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
  {\large \textbf{Luis E. Mart\'{i}nez-Rodr\'{i}guez, Ph.D.}}\\[0.5ex]
  \normalsize
  Boston, MA \,\textbar\, US Work Authorized \,\textbar\, luised94@mit.edu \\
  \href{https://linkedin.com/in/luis-martinez-1394b619a}{linkedin.com/in/luis-martinez-1394b619a} \,\textbar\, \href{https://github.com/luised94}{github.com/luised94}
\end{center}

\cvsection{Summary}

Postdoctoral researcher with expertise in protein biochemistry, yeast genetics, and genomic data analysis. Designed a genetic screen, characterized hits biochemically using reconstituted multi-protein systems, and built a ChIP-seq computational pipeline from scratch to map genome-wide effects. Seeking scientist roles in biotech/pharma.

\cvsection{Skills}

\textbf{Biochemistry:} protein purification (bacterial/yeast, multi-subunit complexes), reconstituted eukaryotic replication assays, EMSA, ATPase assays, fluorescence polarization

\textbf{Genomics:} ChIP-seq (sample preparation through peak calling and motif analysis), whole-genome sequencing, variant calling, NGS library preparation

\textbf{Genetics:} genetic screen design and validation, CRISPR-Cas9 (yeast + mammalian), AID2 degron, tetrad dissection, bulk-segregant analysis

\textbf{Computation:} R (Gviz, genomic visualization), Bash (NGS pipelines on Linux HPC: Bowtie2, SAMtools, deeptools), Python, Git/GitHub

\cvsection{Experience}

\uline{MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY (MIT)}, Cambridge, MA
\vspace{-0.1em}

\textbf{Post-doctoral Researcher,} Bell Lab, MIT Biology \hfill 2023--present

- Designed and executed ChIP-seq experiments to characterize genomic occupancy of wild-type and mutant origin recognition complex across the yeast genome
- Built ChIP-seq analysis pipeline from scratch (alignment, contamination filtering, signal normalization, peak calling, motif analysis) on Linux HPC, establishing a new genomic capability in the lab
- Implemented auxin-inducible degron (AID2) system for conditional depletion of essential replication factors, enabling acute loss-of-function experiments not previously possible in the lab

\textbf{Graduate Researcher,} Bell Lab, MIT Biology \hfill 2016--2023

- Designed and validated a genetic screen for suppressors of a lethal ORC ATPase mutation, then analyzed whole-genome sequencing data to identify causal variants
- Engineered suppressor alleles using CRISPR-Cas9 and characterized their genetic interactions through allele-specificity and drug-sensitivity assays
- Reconstituted wild-type and mutant ORC into multi-protein replication complexes to biochemically characterize the effects of suppressor mutations on DNA replication initiation \textit{in vitro}

\textbf{Research Intern,} MIT/Pfizer Internship Program (Course 7.930[J]) \hfill 2022

- Operated the Beacon Optofluidic System for high-throughput single-cell screening of CRISPR-edited primary human cells
- Developed immunofluorescent screening assay to identify therapeutic targets for cystic fibrosis in collagen-producing primary cells
- Electroporated CRISPR-Cas9-gRNA ribonucleoprotein complexes into primary human cells and optimized culture conditions for assay compatibility

\cvsection{Education}

Ph.D. in Biology, Massachusetts Institute of Technology, 2023 \\
B.Sc. in Cellular and Molecular Biology, University of Puerto Rico-R\'{i}o Piedras, 2016

\cvsection{Publications}

\textbf{Mart\'{i}nez-Rodr\'{i}guez, L.} \& Bell, S. P. (2026). A lethal ORC ATPase mutation is suppressed by alterations in ORC subunits and RNA Pol II transcription components. \textit{Submitted.}

\textbf{Mart\'{i}nez-Rodr\'{i}guez, L.}, Erdogan, O., Jimenez-Rodriguez, M., Gonzalez-Rivera, K., Williams, T., Li, L., \& Carter, C. W. (2015). Functional Class I and II Amino Acid-activating Enzymes Can Be Coded by Opposite Strands of the Same Gene. \textit{Journal of Biological Chemistry}, 290(32), 19710--19725.
```
